food_subgroup: Noodle soup dish — flat rice noodles (kway teow) in clear pork rib and dried sole fish broth with toppings (pork slices, fishballs, fried shallots, spring onion, chili)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for koay teow th'ng specifically. Closest parent concept: noodle soup. FoodOn does not currently index Penang Teochew noodle soup dishes at this specificity.
ontology.foodex2_description: Noodle soup with meat and fish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for koay teow th'ng. Closest parent category: Noodle soup with meat and fish. Code A0175 used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for this traditional Malaysian noodle soup dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker bowl of koay teow th'ng weighing approximately 520g, comprising roughly 200g flat rice noodles, 270g clear pork rib and dried sole fish broth, and 50g of toppings including pork slices, fishballs, fried shallots, spring onion, and chili.

nutrient_source.title: Koay Teow Th'ng — IMR Malaysia MyFCD (koay teow soup / flat rice noodle soup entry)
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research Malaysia — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry for koay teow soup (flat rice noodle soup). MyFCD is an approved primary regional source for Malaysian dishes under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rice Noodles and Analogous Flat Noodle Products — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~58 estimated for flat rice noodles (kway teow) in clear broth based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for rice noodles and flat rice noodle products (GI range 53–61 for boiled flat rice noodles). Flat noodle structure and clear broth (no fat-rich coconut milk) support a medium GI estimate. GL per 520g bowl calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (58 × 57.2) / 100 ≈ 33.2. Confidence: 0.9 (analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for koay teow th'ng in published literature).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — approximately 2,496mg per 520g bowl (480mg per 100g), primarily from the clear pork rib and dried sole fish broth
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request 'kurang sup' (less broth) to reduce sodium intake by approximately 400–600mg per serving; avoid adding dark soy sauce or chili sauce at the table, and consider this dish only occasionally as even with broth reduction the sodium load remains clinically significant.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load of approximately 33 per bowl driven by 200g flat rice noodles; GI ~58 (medium) but large noodle portion elevates GL to clinically significant level
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should request a half-portion of noodles (approximately 100g) to reduce GL to approximately 16, which is clinically acceptable; the clear broth (no coconut milk, low fat) is a relative advantage over other Penang noodle soups, making this the most diabetes-reasonable Penang noodle soup option when portion-controlled.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (2,496mg per bowl) poses cardiovascular risk; however, low saturated fat (0.5g per 100g; ~2.6g per bowl) and zero trans fat are favourable relative to fried or coconut milk-based noodle dishes
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption frequency due to high sodium load; the low saturated fat and absence of trans fat make this a relatively better noodle soup choice compared to Penang Hokkien Mee or laksa, but sodium management through broth reduction remains essential.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork rib broth provides collagen precursors (glycine, proline); fishballs provide modest omega-3; piperine from white pepper may enhance selenium and B12 absorption; sodium remains the primary concern
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Koay teow th'ng may be consumed by rheumatoid arthritis patients for its collagen precursor content from pork rib broth, but sodium intake should be managed by requesting less broth.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Koay Teow Th'ng is a traditional Penang Teochew Chinese clear rice noodle soup, widely consumed as a breakfast and lunch dish at Penang hawker centres and food courts. The dish consists of flat rice noodles (kway teow) served in a clear, amber-coloured broth made by simmering pork ribs with dried sole fish (ikan senangin kering) and sometimes dried squid for 2–3 hours, then straining to produce a clean, savoury stock. Toppings typically include thin-sliced pork, fishballs (processed fish paste), fried shallots, spring onion, and fresh or pickled chili. White pepper is added at the table. Per 520g bowl: approximately 374 kcal, 21.8g protein, 7.8g fat, 57.2g carbohydrate, and a very high sodium load of approximately 2,496mg. GI ~58 (medium), GL ~33 per bowl (HIGH — clinically significant). The dish is dairy-free and contains no coconut milk, making it lighter than laksa or curry mee. It is considered the most hypertension-reasonable and diabetes-reasonable Penang noodle soup when portion-controlled. Sodium management (requesting less broth) is the single most impactful modification for patients with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Nutrient data sourced from IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Confidence: HIGH.
common_user_queries: Penang clear noodle soup nutrition facts

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from IMR Malaysia MyFCD, an approved primary regional source for Malaysian dishes. The MyFCD entry for koay teow soup (flat rice noodle soup) is an exact match. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous rice noodle entries with documented basis. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are populated from the verified source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for koay teow th'ng not reported in IMR Malaysia MyFCD as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content not separately reported in the MyFCD entry for this dish. trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per MyFCD source — no hydrogenated fats used in traditional preparation). All other micronutrients populated from MyFCD verified data. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database rice noodle entries (GI range 53–61 for boiled flat rice noodles); no direct GI measurement for koay teow th'ng exists in published literature. GL per bowl = (58 × 57.2) / 100 ≈ 33.2. This dish is a Malaysian Penang Teochew dish; multilingual names include Chinese (粿条汤 / 粿條湯), Malay (Sup Koay Teow), Tamil (கோய் தியோ தாங்), and Hindi (कोय तियो थंग) as required for Singaporean-context dishes. The dish is not halal due to pork content. Fishballs may contain gluten depending on manufacturer; allergen flagged accordingly.

Fixed Hindi aliases_by_language to use consistent Devanagari script ('कोय तियो थंग (पेनांग क्लियर राइस नूडल सूप)', 'कोय तियो थंग') and added both to flat aliases.
